feedback.text: Hello, I am writing this feedback to bring attention to the fact that the Digital Fairness Act should also include protection for consumers of digital purchases from game publishers who decide to discontinue a game once it no longer generates profit. It has happened to me repeatedly that I purchased in-game items for real money (), only for the game to be shut down laterleaving me with no access to the digital purchases I paid for. I find this not only unfair and disappointing as a customer who spent hard-earned money, but also from the perspective of someone who believes in the cultural value and preservation of video games. Many people hesitate to call video games art because they are mostly associated with lighthearted fun. But if musicians compose music, artists design characters and environments, and writers craft stories for these games, then (in my opinion) it only makes sense to recognize video games as art. That being said, I believe we should protect those who paid for games and made digital purchasesthis includes mobile games, which are often overlooked and, ironically, most frequently shut down. Many of these games generated significant revenue through digital purchases, only for publishers to later decide that consumers no longer have the right to access what they paid for.
